countries.summary: Mexico's government is advancing Plan B electoral reforms while reducing pharmaceutical debt from 19 billion to 5 billion pesos during AMLO's administration. The country faces ongoing security challenges with military confrontations against drug cartels increasing 26%, alongside infrastructure incidents including a deadly Pemex refinery fire.
headlines: Government reduces debt with pharmaceutical companies during AMLO administration; decreases from 19 billion to 5 billion pesos: Eduardo Clark
Fire at Dos Bocas leaves 5 dead: Pemex; assures incident is under control
Military confrontations against drug cartels increase 26%
Plan B proposes that Executive head can promote recall vote; initiative foresees exercise between 2027 and 2028
Pemex workers maintain sit-in on Marina Nacional; demand salary adjustment

countries.summary: Chile's new ultra-conservative President José Antonio Kast has taken office promising a "Government of emergency" focused on fiscal austerity, border security, and economic restructuring. Kast's administration signals a sharp rightward turn from the previous leftist government, with early moves including migration controls, fiscal audits, and plans to limit university fee waivers while collecting student debts.
headlines: Kast seeks to limit university fee waivers and collect student debts from private banks guaranteed by the State
Kast announces 'National Reconstruction' project including temporary elimination of VAT on housing
Kast signs decrees for fiscal audit in his first hours in office
Kast: "There really is no money" - calls for help from Chileans in early days
Chile approaches US sphere of influence in critical minerals and rare earths race

countries.summary: Brazil faces significant fiscal challenges as the government considers temporary ICMS tax reductions on diesel fuel while the BNDES plans new strategic credit lines amid global uncertainties. Political tensions continue with corruption investigations targeting lawmakers and former officials, while economic indicators show mixed signals with dollar weakness and moderate stock gains.
headlines: Finance Ministry calls states to discuss temporary reduction of ICMS tax on diesel fuel
BNDES plans new credit line to strengthen strategic sectors affected by wars
US eases sanctions on Venezuela to release more oil amid war with Iran
Dollar closes down with reduced global risk aversion and Stock Exchange has moderate gains after future interest rates reverse decline
Deputies convicted by STF for 'selling' amendments receive 5 to 6 years prison sentences unanimously

countries.summary: Panama's government is managing significant fiscal pressures, with the Ministry of Finance seeking $12 billion in financing to cover liquidity needs while authorizing $18.8 million for electrical grid stabilization in western regions. The country shows mixed economic signals with 4.4% GDP growth in 2025 and 8-10% increased Canal revenues, though political investigations and corruption cases continue to create uncertainty.
headlines: MEF will manage financing of $12 billion to cover liquidity needs
Panamanian economy grew 4.4% in 2025
Canal registers 8% to 10% more revenue from increased ship traffic
Cabinet Council authorizes disbursement of $18.8 million for electrical stabilization of the West
Aaron Mizrachi must face trial in Odebrecht case for alleged money laundering

countries.summary: Peru is experiencing significant political transition with Luis Arroyo Sánchez sworn in as new Prime Minister and Rodolfo Acuña as new Economy Minister, indicating potential policy shifts ahead of 2026 elections. The government announces a S/500 million injection for state oil company Petro-Perú, while 23 presidential candidates present economic proposals including tax exemptions and formalization incentives with elections approaching.
headlines: Luis Arroyo Sánchez sworn in as new Prime Minister
Rodolfo Acuña sworn in as new Economy Minister
Government announces S/500 million injection for state oil company Petro-Perú
23 presidential candidates present economic proposals including tax exemptions and formalization incentives
Carlos Álvarez proposes income tax exemption for small industries that formalize

countries.summary: Uruguay's new government under President Yamandú Orsi and Finance Minister Gabriel Oddone is pushing new legislation and measures to improve competitiveness and attract investments. The country faces economic challenges with authorities focusing on fiscal anchoring strategies and risk management scenarios for the economy.
headlines: New bill and measures for Uruguay to improve competitiveness, according to the MEF
What is the government's strategy for a crucial aspect of the Uruguayan economy that determines the fiscal "anchor"?
Materialized risks for Uruguay: the economy in a worse scenario
BPS: number of foreign workers in Uruguay exceeded 100,000 for the first time in history
Congress of Mayors approved 50% reduction in traffic fines considered "less serious"

countries.summary: Ecuador continues implementing a nationwide curfew with over 380 arrests in two days as the government intensifies its fight against organized crime and drug trafficking. The country is advancing cooperation programs with the US and EU on security matters while managing weather-related agricultural concerns and maintaining fiscal operations through tax collection efforts.
headlines: Tax Authority sends over 600,000 notifications to taxpayers to correct errors in time and avoid fines
Company debuts in securities market placing 100% of $5 million bond issuance
Ecuador and European Union expand border security and cooperation program
Cooperation with US begins to show results: FBI agreement, trade accord, security operations mark agenda
Immediate measures for rains 'significantly reduced Fusarium dispersion potential'

countries.summary: El Salvador has approved constitutional reforms enabling life imprisonment for homicides, rape, and terrorism cases, as pushed by President Nayib Bukele's administration. The country continues to showcase its culture internationally while maintaining domestic infrastructure development and job creation initiatives.
headlines: El Salvador approves life imprisonment for murderers, rapists and terrorists
Central Bank of El Salvador (BCR) opens more than 300 job positions with available salaries and positions
New residential complex delivered to relocated families in San Salvador
Vamos party discards presidential candidacy and redefines strategy
El Salvador shines with culture and pupusas in New York

countries.summary: Argentina faces mounting political scandals as President Milei confronts corruption allegations involving a $5 million cryptocurrency scheme while his approval ratings face pressure from rising inflation. The opposition is intensifying judicial pressure on Milei's administration amid questions about government integrity and economic policy effectiveness.
headlines: Inflation in Argentina exceeds Milei's forecasts and reaches 33% year-on-year
Argentine Justice finds alleged $5 million agreement for Milei to promote $Libra cryptocurrency scam
Major Argentine businessmen warn about critical industry situation and demand respect from Milei
Milei seeks investors in New York and calls Argentine businessmen thieves
Milei reinforces his alliance with the "judicial party" surrounded by scandals

countries.summary: The Dominican Republic appears to be managing routine government operations with ongoing infrastructure projects and educational reforms, while maintaining fiscal discipline through canceled procurement processes. The country faces some regional challenges from neighboring Haiti's instability and energy sector maintenance issues, but overall political and economic conditions appear stable.
headlines: Inabie cancels tender for purchasing multivitamin gummies for students
Government presents spaceport project in Pedernales; company says it won't cost "a penny" to DR
Fed begins meeting on interest rates amid crossroads due to war with Iran
Oil prices rise again due to war in Iran
Banking sector will facilitate digital mortgage contracting starting in April

countries.summary: Colombia faces significant fiscal challenges with its budget deficit reaching the highest level in three decades, while President Petro escalates diplomatic tensions with Ecuador over alleged bombing incidents. The country is also preparing for the 2026 presidential elections amid political polarization and ongoing security concerns.
headlines: Petro Government's Financial Plan Recognizes Delicate Fiscal Moment - Deficit Reaches Highest Level in Three Decades

Mexico, Colombia and Brazil Call for "Immediate Ceasefire" in Iran War
Colombia and Venezuela Reschedule Bilateral Meeting for Late April in Maracaibo
2026 Presidential Election Campaigns Intensify as Candidates Announce Vice-Presidential Formulas

countries.summary: Paraguay faces significant fiscal challenges as the Treasury acknowledges funding shortages despite stable economic conditions, with the Finance Minister announcing spending cuts to avoid financial crisis. Political tensions rise over pension system reforms and various domestic issues while regional security concerns persist with drug trafficking cases.
headlines: "The economy is well, the Treasury is bad," what Valdovinos admitted regarding lack of funds

New setback in consumer confidence index

countries.summary: Bolivia's President Paz announced a new "50-50" hydrocarbon law to attract investment and boost regional development, while also signing multiple cooperation agreements with Brazil including potential partnerships with Petrobras. The country is facing ongoing security challenges with the high-profile capture and extradition of drug trafficker Sebastián Marset, alongside preparations for upcoming subnational elections on March 22.
headlines: Paz announces new Hydrocarbon Law with "50-50" scheme to attract investment and boost regional development
President of Bolivia wants to "relaunch" relationship with Brazilian oil company Petrobras

countries.summary: Costa Rica faces significant fiscal challenges as lawmakers advance pension increases for higher-income retirees, creating additional budget pressures on the social security system. The exchange rate continues declining to historic lows below ₡470 per dollar, while analysts monitor Federal Reserve and ECB interest rate decisions amid Iran conflict tensions.

Analysts point out that the Fed and ECB will maintain interest rates in their first meeting after the Iran conflict

countries.summary: Guatemala's Congress approved pension reforms requiring IPM retirees to receive minimum wage benefits while mandating financial sustainability studies. Political tensions persist over Constitutional Court renewal processes amid legal challenges and international oversight concerns.

DIACO presents 37 new complaints for fuel price speculation

countries.summary: Honduras faces ongoing political tensions with electoral disputes and security challenges including gang violence and extortion activities affecting transportation sectors. The country is dealing with infrastructure concerns following a fatal airline accident while managing local economic pressures from rising food prices ahead of Easter season.
